question: How do I reset my password?
answer: To reset your password, go to the login page and click 'Forgot Password'. Enter your registered email address and we'll send you a reset link. The link expires in 30 minutes. If you don't receive the email, check your spam folder or contact support.

question: How does billing work? When am I charged?
answer: CloudSync bills on a monthly or annual cycle depending on your plan. You are charged at the beginning of each billing period. You can view your invoices and payment history in Settings > Billing. We accept all major credit cards and PayPal.

question: How do I cancel my subscription?
answer: To cancel your subscription, go to Settings > Billing > Manage Subscription and click 'Cancel Plan'. Your access continues until the end of the current billing period. No refunds are issued for partial months. You can reactivate anytime.

question: How do I delete my account?
answer: To delete your account, go to Settings > Account > Delete Account. You will be asked to confirm by typing your email. Account deletion is permanent and removes all your data within 30 days. Please export your data before deleting your account.

question: How can I export my data from CloudSync?
answer: You can export your data by going to Settings > Data Management > Export Data. Choose the format (JSON or CSV) and the data types you want to export. The export is processed in the background and you'll receive a download link via email when it's ready.

question: How do I add or remove team members?
answer: Team management is available on the Pro and Enterprise plans. Go to Settings > Team > Members to invite new users by email or remove existing ones. You can assign roles (Admin, Editor, Viewer) to control access levels. Each additional seat is billed according to your plan.

question: How do I set up integrations with other tools?
answer: CloudSync integrates with Slack, Jira, GitHub, Google Drive, and more. Go to Settings > Integrations to browse available integrations. Click 'Connect' on the integration you want and follow the OAuth authorization flow. Each integration has its own configuration options.

question: Is there a mobile app for CloudSync?
answer: Yes! CloudSync has native apps for iOS and Android. Download them from the App Store or Google Play. The mobile app supports push notifications, offline access to recent documents, and full sync capabilities. Sign in with your existing CloudSync credentials.

question: How do I enable two-factor authentication (2FA)?
answer: To enable 2FA, go to Settings > Security > Two-Factor Authentication and click 'Enable'. You can use an authenticator app (like Google Authenticator or Authy) or receive SMS codes. We strongly recommend using an authenticator app for better security. Save your backup codes in a safe place.

question: What pricing plans are available?
answer: CloudSync offers three plans: Free (up to 3 projects, 1GB storage), Pro ($12/month per user, unlimited projects, 50GB storage, priority support), and Enterprise (custom pricing, unlimited everything, dedicated support, SSO, audit logs). Annual billing saves 20%.

question: What is CloudSync's uptime SLA?
answer: CloudSync guarantees 99.9% uptime for Pro plans and 99.99% for Enterprise plans. Our status page at status.cloudsync.example.com shows real-time and historical uptime data. If we fail to meet the SLA, affected customers receive service credits automatically.

question: Does CloudSync offer API access?
answer: Yes, CloudSync provides a RESTful API for Pro and Enterprise plans. You can generate API keys in Settings > Developer > API Keys. The API supports all core operations including file management, user management, and sync triggers. Rate limits are 100 requests/minute for Pro and 1000/minute for Enterprise. Full API documentation is available at docs.cloudsync.example.com/api.

question: How do I contact CloudSync support?
answer: You can reach our support team through several channels: in-app live chat (Pro and Enterprise), email at support@cloudsync.example.com, or our community forum at community.cloudsync.example.com. Pro users get priority support with 4-hour response time. Enterprise users get a dedicated account manager and 1-hour response time.

question: Is my data encrypted and secure?
answer: Yes, CloudSync takes security seriously. All data is encrypted at rest using AES-256 and in transit using TLS 1.3. We perform regular third-party security audits and penetration testing. Enterprise plans include additional security features like SSO (SAML/OIDC), IP allowlisting, and audit logs.

question: Can I upgrade or downgrade my plan?
answer: Yes, you can change your plan anytime in Settings > Billing > Change Plan. Upgrades take effect immediately and you'll be charged a prorated amount. Downgrades take effect at the start of your next billing cycle. If you downgrade and exceed the lower plan's limits, you'll need to reduce usage before the change takes effect.
